Instead of chasing members, your group gets discovered by people already searching for support.

 How to grow your support group
 Tips to improve attendance and engagement
 Real strategies from group leaders [...] # Welcome to MentalHappy — the platform for running support groups like real healthcare programs.
## MentalHappy | Online Support Groups 
77 subscribers
4 likes

### Description
50 views
Posted: 14 Apr 2026
Running a support group isn’t just about leading sessions—it’s about filling them.

If you're a therapist, coach, advocate, or someone with lived experience, you already know how to guide conversations. The real challenge is building consistent attendance and keeping your group growing week after week.

Posting links, sharing on social media, and emailing your list only goes so far. Without the right system, filling your group can feel unpredictable—and that impacts your ability to help others. [...] You support others for a living. Maybe you are a therapist, coach, advocate, or someone with lived experience who believes in the power of support groups. You have the experience to lead, but filling your group consistently can feel like a constant struggle. Many leaders are great at guiding conversations, but struggle to fill seats consistently. You post on social media, email your list, and share links everywhere, yet attendance still feels unpredictable. When turnout is low, your impact shrinks and momentum fades, making it harder to build a reliable community. At Mental Happy, we change that by helping your group get discovered by people already searching for support. Instead of chasing members, we make it easy for the right people to find your group at the right moment in a space

results.title: Find Online Support Groups Near You — Free to Join
results.content:
Can I find support groups near me on MentalHappy? +

MentalHappy groups are fully online — available wherever you are, no local search required. Online also means access to far more topics and leaders than you'd find in your city, without the commute.

## Your group is already out there. Let's find it.

Whatever you're carrying, you don't have to figure it out alone. Join free and find your people.

Join Free — Find Your Group

No credit card. No referral. No waiting. Takes 60 seconds.

.svg)

Run and join support groups like real healthcare programs, not Zoom meetings.

ARPA-H Spoke Member

San Francisco, CA 94115

About UsBlogPartner With us

For Leaders

Host a Group

For Members

Find a GroupResourcesDownload the App [...] See all FAQs →

Are online support groups on MentalHappy private? +

Yes. Every member signs a confidentiality agreement before joining. You can also use our anonymous participation feature for extra privacy. Your conversations are never shared outside the group, and the platform is fully HIPAA-compliant.

What actually happens in a session? +

Sessions are led by a trained professional — a therapist, counselor, or certified coach. Each includes guided discussion, reflection, and practical tools. Groups are small and intimate, usually 5 to 15 people, on a regular schedule.

Do I have to talk? Can I just listen? +

Not at all. Many members start by just listening. You'll never be put on the spot or called on. Share when — and if — you're ready.

Is it really free to join? + [...] Is it really free to join? +

Creating your account is completely free. Groups range from free to low-cost — typically $5–$25 per session, with many donation-based options. You'll always see pricing before you commit to anything.

Do I need a therapist referral? +

No referral needed. Anyone can sign up. Many members come through therapist recommendations, but MentalHappy is designed to complement your existing care — not gatekeep it.

What if there's no group for what I'm going through? +

Request one. Submit a group request and we'll find a qualified leader to build it. You'll be notified the moment it launches.

Can I find support groups near me on MentalHappy? +

Find answers to your group questions here.

What type of groups can I run or host on MentalHappy?

answer:

You can host any non-clinical, psycho-educational group related to health, mental health, wellness, or faith. This includes groups that provide education, facilitate engaging discussions, share resources, or support people through life events and challenges. If your group helps members grow, connect, and improve their wellbeing, it’s welcome on MentalHappy.

How does this platform work?

answer: [...] Who uses MentalHappy to host groups?

answer:

Many types of leaders and organizations host groups on MentalHappy — including therapists, coaches, healthcare professionals, mental health advocates, peer supporters, nonprofit organizations, schools, hospitals, government agencies, and community groups. Some use MentalHappy to expand their private practice, some run coaching or wellness programs, and others lead peer-support or survivor-based groups. Whether you’re helping people through life changes, health challenges, relationships, or personal growth — MentalHappy makes it simple to offer psycho-educational group support

Can I host a free support group?

answer:

Yes — you can host free or donation-based groups. You can also collect donations through the app. [...] Does MentalHappy have its own video integration?

answer:

Yes, video sessions can be hosted within the platform.

Why are breakout rooms not available in MentalHappy Video sessions?

answer:

To maintain group cohesion and trust, MentalHappy does not use breakout rooms for its support groups

Can I promote my group externally?

answer:

Yes, share your group link via email, social media, or newsletters
